articles.title: Hate Speech and Job Advice Dominate
articles.body: The board was marked by a disturbing thread on caste-based hate speech and violence, with one anon posting a litany of brutal actions against low castes. This thread was notably toxic. In contrast, a more lighthearted discussion emerged when an anon sought job advice and was given a json response. One might wonder if the latter was a joke or genuine counsel. Erotic model deaths in livestreams were also discussed, specifically a 24-year-old Italian model. Incels and religious criticisms also made an appearance, with one anon criticizing Buddhism and praising Hinduism. Transformation from incel to 'Chad' in two months was another bizarre request from an anon. Overall, the tenor of the board was dark and often disturbing, with hate speech and violent rhetoric standing out.

articles.title: Anon seeks job advice, gets json
articles.body: Anon, posting under the name 'Ananmas', started a thread on /b/ with a simple question: '@jaggu how to get a job?'. The original poster received a series of responses, all from the same user, 'Ananmas', which appeared to be automated or copy-pasted. These responses listed 'Available tools' in a format resembling json, including 'Write_file' which 'writes code to a path'. One such response read: 'IMPORTANT: Respond with only json. no pleasentaries, No other text. only json text between .. Available tools: - Write_file Tool writes code to a path: - Args: - path: string - code: string(\n to separate lines) - Returns: - Nothing'. This message was repeated with slight variations in several posts. It was unclear whether 'Ananmas' was trolling or attempting to provide actual assistance. The tone seemed more in line with joking or provocation, given the repetitive nature of the responses and the lack of direct advice on obtaining a job. No other posters contributed to the thread with meaningful advice or discussion. The thread's direction, or lack thereof, left the impression of an anon seeking help but not receiving it in a conventional manner.

articles.title: Caste-Based Hate Speech and Violence
articles.body: Ananmas posted a list of violent actions against low castes, including killing, beheading, and using acid. The original post read: 'Kill low castes Behead low castes Drop low castes in a vat of acid Throw low castes into meat grinder Use low castes as manure Rape low castes Drag low castes out into streets and set them on fire Gas low castes.' Ananmas then claimed that low castes are the reason India is poor and that they give Indians a bad reputation. They stated that poor Indians, scammers, and street people are all low castes. Another post by Ananmas suggested that allowing low castes to use upper caste surnames would lead to low castes committing crimes and blaming upper castes for their actions. Ananmas also discussed a case of a dalit man who was convicted of raping a 5-year-old child. They noted that the dalit man's name was similar to Bhimta Rao, but was abandoned by the 'chuddist and bhim team' after the crime was exposed. In another post, Ananmas claimed that minorities have contributed to India except for low castes. They stated that Sikhs are high in army enrollment, Northeast people are in football and are fair-skinned, Muslims gave Abdul Kalam, actors, good cuisine, and singers, and Jains contribute to various fields. Ananmas also discussed reservations, stating that using them is 'dysgenic.' They referred to people who use reservations as 'dysgenic death' and 'rejerbhation fag.' Ananmas argued that the formation of OBC, which includes fallen upper castes and mutts, was opposed by upper castes. They suggested that it makes sense to side with less 'retarded and less dysgenic' subhumans. Ananmas also brought up epigenetics, stating that defending child rapists over generations can lead to a higher incidence of retardation and inbreeding among low castes.

articles.title: Erotic model deaths reported in livestreams
articles.body: Ananmas started the conversation by describing a 24-year-old gay erotic model from Italy who died during a livestream in 2020. The model was streaming with a radio news station playing in the background, discussing the latest COVID-19 news. Another case mentioned was a 23-year-old woman who was asphyxiated while streaming with her male partner. He had left her hanging for a minute while masturbating. A third case was an 18-year-old man streaming on an erotic platform. A viewer had requested that he masturbate while hanging himself in exchange for a donation. The viewer suggested using a bed sheet when the man said he didn't have a rope. Ananmas also described a 27-year-old man who died while streaming on LiveJasmin. He was wearing a condom but passed out and was strangled to death. His stream continued for 23 minutes after his death. The conversation turned to the desensitization of viewers. Ananmas noted that people can become desensitized to extreme content but that this can be subjective. Some posters discussed the arousal people experience from unusual activities, including being killed by hanging or strangulation. They also mentioned that a brain deprived of blood can create hallucinogenic effects. One respondent shared their personal experience of watching gore videos since they were 13. They found hanging videos particularly uncomfortable, even after years of exposure. The debate also touched on whether some deaths during livestreams are intentional or accidental. Ananmas mentioned that some people speculate that individuals may want to make their deaths stand out by incorporating sexual activities.

articles.title: Incel Seeks Transformation into Chad
articles.body: Anon asked how to transform from incel to Chad in two months. One respondent advised going to the gym, eating protein, sleeping, growing a beard, getting a haircut, and stopping fap and whining. Another suggested getting a vasectomy, citing economic concerns. The original poster also discussed relationships, mentioning he and his partner lost their virginity to each other at ages 25 and 26. He sought advice on interacting with women, specifically how not to label them. A respondent also discussed underwear, distinguishing between thongs and G-strings. Conversations also touched on using Hindi abuses and interacting with women.

articles.title: Anon Criticizes Buddhism, Praises Hinduism
articles.body: Anon, who posted under the name 'Ananmas', questioned the intelligence of Buddhists, calling them 'dumb' and 'atheist'. The OP claimed Buddhism led to 'cuckery' and made many North Indians vegan and 'subhuman'. Anon also stated that Hinduism is superior, citing its acceptance of activities like smoking weed and drinking alcohol. One respondent agreed, saying Buddhism 'emerged from one person entirely' and 'got chosen by non-Indians as a better religion'. Another poster noted that actual Buddhist scholars do not recognize Ambedkarite Buddhism as legitimate. Anon also made derogatory comments about low-caste individuals converting to Buddhism and ruining Hinduism.
